name: Point2d
summary: The Point2d class allows you to work with a point in 2D space. Point2d is a series of values representing x and y coordinates. The values are specified as [x, y]. For example [1, 1]. To create a point call Geom::Point2d.new, where the creation method can take a variety of arguments:

constructors.name: initialize
constructors.summary: The new method creates a new Geom::Point2d.
constructors.remarks: The new method creates a new Geom::Point2d.

params.name: x
params.doc: The location along the x axis

params.name: y
params.doc: The location along the y axis

methods.name: +
methods.summary: The #+ operator is a simple way to add to the current x and y values of the Geom::Point2d, or to set the values of the Geom::Point2d by adding a Vector2d to the Geom::Point2d.
methods.remarks: The #+ operator is a simple way to add to the current x and y values of the Geom::Point2d, or to set the values of the Geom::Point2d by adding a Vector2d to the Geom::Point2d.

methods.summary: The #- operator is a simple way to subtract from the current x and y values of the Geom::Point2d.
methods.remarks: The #- operator is a simple way to subtract from the current x and y values of the Geom::Point2d.

methods.name: clone
methods.summary: The #clone method creates another point identical to the Geom::Point2d being cloned.
methods.remarks: The #clone method creates another point identical to the Geom::Point2d being cloned.

methods.name: distance
methods.summary: The #distance method computes the distance from the Geom::Point2d to another Geom::Point2d.
methods.remarks: The #distance method computes the distance from the Geom::Point2d to another Geom::Point2d.

returns.doc: the distance between the points in the current units

methods.name: inspect
methods.summary: The #inspect method formats the Geom::Point2d as a string.
methods.remarks: The #inspect method formats the Geom::Point2d as a string.

methods.name: offset
methods.summary: The #offset method offsets the Geom::Point2d by a Vector2d and returns a new Geom::Point2d.
methods.remarks: The #offset method offsets the Geom::Point2d by a Vector2d and returns a new Geom::Point2d. If distance is provided, it must be non-zero.

methods.name: offset!
methods.summary: The #offset! method offsets the Geom::Point2d by a Vector2d.
methods.remarks: The #offset! method offsets the Geom::Point2d by a Vector2d. The Geom::Point2d itself is modified. The length of the vector must not be zero.

methods.name: set!
methods.summary: The #set! method sets the values of the Geom::Point2d.
methods.remarks: The #set! method sets the values of the Geom::Point2d.

methods.name: to_a
methods.summary: The #to_a method converts the Geom::Point2d to an array of 2 numbers.
methods.remarks: The #to_a method converts the Geom::Point2d to an array of 2 numbers.

returns.doc: an array of two numbers representing x, y of the Geom::Point2d

methods.name: to_s
methods.summary: The #to_s method returns a string representation of the Geom::Point2d.
methods.remarks: The #to_s method returns a string representation of the Geom::Point2d.

methods.name: transform
methods.summary: The #transform method applies a transformation to a point, returning a new point.
methods.remarks: The #transform method applies a transformation to a point, returning a new point. The original point is unchanged by this method.

params.name: transform
params.doc: A Transformation object to apply to the point.

methods.name: transform!
methods.summary: The #transform! method applies a transformation to a point.
methods.remarks: The #transform! method applies a transformation to a point. The point itself is modified.

params.name: transform
params.doc: A Transformation object to apply to the point.

methods.name: vector_to
methods.summary: The #vector_to method returns the vector between points.
methods.remarks: The #vector_to method returns the vector between points.

properties.summary: The #== method compares two points for equality.
properties.remarks: The #== method compares two points for equality. This uses the standard SketchUp tolerance to determine if two points are the same.

properties.summary: The #[] method returns the value of the Geom::Point2d at the specified index.
properties.remarks: The #[] method returns the value of the Geom::Point2d at the specified index.

properties.name: x
properties.summary: The #x method returns the x value of the Geom::Point2d.
properties.remarks: The #x method returns the x value of the Geom::Point2d.

properties.name: y
properties.summary: The #y method returns the y value of the Geom::Point2d.
properties.remarks: The #y method returns the y value of the Geom::Point2d.
